definition: A critical conceptual unit within the top term domain of the Exegesis. Mentions: 226.
interpretive_note:
Primary Source Evidence: "...C-35

Thus we are not to dominate or exploit nature but to become part of it as the Corpus Christi - Christ and the ecosphere are homological (equated); he has invaded it and transubstantiated it into himself in a dynamic process in which his rationality (Logos) overcomes its irrational will. It has a mind (Noos) and he is both it as [] and it as psyche/noos. Hence I was quite right to see Teilhar..."

evidence.text:

folder 84 - 003
-1-

I was too tired (wasted) to write this down last night, but I realized that it is my Eleatic continuum view of reality that permits me to see purposeful changes in the reality field, rather than mechanistic cause-and-effect derived from the discontinuous view; thus my Tagore vision --"the ensouling of the animals"-- is of necessity linked to my unusual mechanics/physics, which is to say everything that I have worked out philosophically, epistemologically, politically

C-30

I can suppose no explanation but the Gnostic one. Creation is not only irrational, it is only semi-real or [] only seeming, "rational," then signifies real; "irrational" signifies irreal. How we, the logical value of Gnostic knowing - Gnosis itself. "Knowing" signifies being able then to counter-distinguish the truly real. (These categories relate to Pan-Indian Doctrines of Maya: the Grand Illusion both inner and outer) What must be realized is that, like hallucinations, the merely seeming

C-31

that our evil actions invade God - we again "crucify" him - and, in true N.T. fashion, he has taken on our sins (in the form of passion and death) voluntarily, this incarnation leading to his suffering and death is not forced on him, he is here for this reason as a protest, to awaken us to what we are doing; viz: if we understand that in wounding the ecosphere we wound God, we may - should thereby - know to cease.

Hence, I see Judeo-Christian themes, here, quite beyond Pan-Indian, and I c

which is to say, decapitated him there in his little jail cell! Dio! And it all happened to me at passover!

And ever since the eleventh grade I now heard the still small (low, murmuring) voice.

Let me say this: if you start with Tagore as the premise and work backward, 2-3-74 - 2-75 et al. Becomes logical: Elijah comes before the second advent to prepare the way - []; not Elijah; the spirit of Elijah! Now, if this be the case re 2-74 - 2-75, was it futile? C-33

The Tagore revelation is so classically Biblical, combining both O.T. and N.T. elements. Calling men to repent, to give up the evil they are [already] doing, warning them that otherwise they will cause God to desert them (typically O.T.); Tagore has voluntarily taken on the sins of the world and it is causing him to suffer and die (the suffering servant, the passion and crucifixion); and the Kerygma itself: now that it is available to me I can see it as the logical extension proj

C-34

not immediately harm us humans. But the new dispensation deals with the entire life-chain as an indivisible whole; viewed this way, this crime assumes new magnitude.

It occurs to me to ask: if, as I saw in 3-74, there is a secret true Christian underground (hence the cypher in "Tears") would it not know of Tagore? One would think so.

Let me ask: Did Jesus' crucifixion possess the effacacy or the news of it? And does Tagore's passion and death in themselves possess effacacy or the news of them? I don't know. And what would the effacacy be? Surely it lies in awakening

C-35

Thus we are not to dominate or exploit nature but to become part of it as the Corpus Christi - Christ and the ecosphere are homological (equated); he has invaded it and transubstantiated it into himself in a dynamic process in which his rationality (Logos) overcomes its irrational will. It has a mind (Noos) and he is both it as [] and it as psyche/noos. Hence I was quite right to see Teilhard's views in all this.
